exemplar.title: How do Kendrick Lamar and Nazrul Islam explore the theme of liberation through their respective lyricism and poetry?

detailedExemplarData.title: How do Kendrick Lamar and Nazrul Islam explore the theme of liberation through their respective lyricism and poetry?

subject.title: English A Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **literary texts from diverse cultures, genres, and time periods**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis, interpretation, and understanding of how literature reflects and shapes human experience through themes, cultural contexts, and global issues**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Unseen literary text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay on studied works | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 40% |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation on global issues | 10 minutes | 10 minutes | 25% |

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, focused, and effectively linked to the ensuing comparative analysis.
The topic is communicated with sustained clarity and purpose throughout the introduction, body sections, and conclusion.
There is a coherent rationale for the comparative study that is revisited consistently, maintaining a clear conceptual thread.
weaknesses: The title is overly long and occasionally vague; it would benefit from greater precision and specificity.
The methodology outline is implicitâselection criteria for texts and analytic procedures are not fully justified or made explicit.
Some biographical and contextual digressions are not always tied back to the central research question, slightly weakening focus.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed the first two focus-and-method strands firmly in the 5-6 band and the methodology strand at the top of the 3-4 band. The clear, focused research question and sustained purpose justify a mark at the upper end of the 5-6 band, but the uneven methodological explanation prevents a full 6.

strengths: Consistent and generally accurate use of subject-specific terminology (e.g., symbolism, enjambment, juxtaposition).
Coherent understanding of both authorsâ historical and cultural contexts, with primary quotations integrated into the analysis.
Effective application of key literary concepts to support interpretive claims.
weaknesses: Occasional misapplications of terms (confusing diction with vulgarity, oversimplifying enjambment).
Factual slips and typographical errors (e.g., âyolkâ for âyokeâ) undermine authority at points.
Reliance on general or popular web sources limits the depth and rigor of the scholarly framework.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both knowledge strands were assessed in the middle (3-4) band. The student demonstrates a solid grasp of terminology and contextual knowledge but makes enough imprecise uses and source limitations to merit a mid-band mark of 4 rather than pushing into the 5-6 band.

strengths: A clear, coherent argument runs throughout, with a conclusion that explicitly answers the research question.
Sustained comparative analysis uncovers meaningful nuances (e.g., collectivism vs. individualism, differing religious allusions).
Strong thematic synthesis passages demonstrate sophisticated critical engagement across both authors.
weaknesses: Overreliance on non-scholarly websites weakens the depth of research and scholarly grounding.
Counter-arguments and alternative perspectives are rarely explored, limiting evaluative breadth.
Analytical depth is uneven in some sections, which slip into descriptive summary rather than fully supported critique.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three critical-thinking strands were judged at the lower end of the 7â9 band. The essayâs consistently relevant research, supported analysis, and focused argument justify an upper-mid-band mark. However, the uneven depth, lack of counter-argument, and reliance on weaker sources prevent a move into the top of the band, so an 8 is appropriate.

strengths: Mandatory structural elements (title page, table of contents, introduction, body sections, conclusion, bibliography) are present and laid out in a logical sequence.
Use of clear subheadings aids navigation through the argument and supports reader orientation.
Consistent font and spacing contribute to overall readability.
weaknesses: Page numbers are missing, and the table-of-contents dot leaders are misaligned, hampering ease of reference.
In-text citations and bibliography entries lack a uniform academic style and completeness.
Heading levels and formatting styles are inconsistent across sections, and several paragraphs end abruptly or mid-sentence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure was solid enough for the lower 3â4 band, but pervasive formatting lapsesâmissing page numbers, inconsistent citation style, misaligned contentsâplace overall presentation at the upper end of the 1â2 band. A mark of 2 reflects clear structure undermined by recurring formatting deficiencies.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The methodology is broadly outlined: two primary texts are selected, and the essay signals that it will analyse portrayals of liberation through literary techniques (symbolism, figurative language, religious allusion). A modest range of secondary sources (journal articles, website articles, dictionary, Bible.com) is cited, showing some informed selection.  

Nonetheless, source use is uneven. Several interpretive claims (e.g., social-historical assertions about sex work, British imperialism, or community transphobia) are presented with limited scholarly support, and citation formatting is inconsistent. Discussion of method (why these particular works were chosen, how they were analysed) remains implicit rather than explicit. These limitations keep the strand within the 3-4 band, meriting the upper mark of 4.

The layout meets several basic IB requirements: consistent font and spacing, inclusion of word count, and overall readability. However, key formatting conventions are missing or inconsistently applied: pages are unnumbered, in-text citations vary in style, some bibliographic entries lack full details, and leader dots in the contents page are incomplete.  

Because only "some" layout considerations are correctly applied and notable deficiencies remain, the work falls in the 1-2 band; a score of 1 reflects the lower end due to multiple recurring formatting lapses.

The primary researchâclose reading of two poems and two songsâis relevant and well integrated with the research question. Secondary sources, though not extensive, are used to contextualise historical background and support interpretive points.  

Gaps remain: several broad claims (e.g., influence on Nobel Prize decisions, community attitudes) lack citation, and some references (dictionary definitions, popular websites) limit scholarly depth. Still, the majority of evidence chosen directly serves the argument, justifying placement in the 7-9 band at the lower end (7).

The student states the topic explicitly in the title and reiterates it in the opening paragraph, immediately signalling an investigation into how two writers explore liberation through short-form literature. A coherent rationale for the comparison (shared concern with liberation across cultures) is articulated and revisited in the major body sections and the conclusion, showing sustained focus.  

Minor shortcomingsâsuch as an over-long, slightly vague title and intermittent lapses in phrasing that obscure meaningâprevent the communication from being outstanding. Nevertheless, purpose and focus remain consistently clear, aligning well with the upper band (5-6).

A clear, coherent argument runs through the essay, culminating in a conclusion that explicitly answers the research question. The student synthesises findings across sections, noting both convergences and divergences between the authors. Occasional evaluative comments (e.g., on societal hypocrisy or limitations of the works) show developing critical reflection.  

However, evaluation is sometimes confined to affirming similarities rather than probing complexities; alternative perspectives or potential counter-readings are rarely explored. Logical flow is occasionally interrupted by minor language errors and abrupt transitions. These factors place the discussion solidly in the 7-9 band but short of the highest levels, warranting a score of 7.

The essay offers sustained comparative analysis of literary techniques and themes, moving beyond surface parallels to examine nuance (e.g., collectivism vs. individualism, differing uses of religion). Quotations are usually unpacked effectively, and links to the research question are maintained.  

Yet, depth is uneven: some sections become descriptive, and certain interpretive leaps (such as psychological motives or cultural generalisations) are asserted rather than demonstrated with evidence. Greater engagement with counter-arguments or scholarly debate would strengthen critique. These qualities indicate solid but not exemplary analytical effectiveness, aligning with a score of 7 in the 7-9 band.

The essay contains all mandatory structural componentsâtitle page, abstract omission (per current guide), table of contents, introduction, body, conclusion, bibliographyâand follows a logical sequence (women, culture, conclusion). Headings support navigation.  

Nevertheless, some structural elements are imperfect: the table of contents lacks leader dots alignment; page numbers are missing, which hampers reference. Paragraphing is occasionally uneven, and transitions between subsections could be smoother. These issues indicate a clear but not flawless structure, fitting the 3-4 band at the lower mark (3).

The essay demonstrates solid knowledge of both writersâ historical contexts and summarises key content from the chosen works. Primary quotations are integrated and generally explained, and there is awareness of relevant socio-political background (British imperialism, U.S. racial dynamics, trans rights debates).  

However, occasional factual slips (e.g., âyolkâ for âyoke,â oversimplified account of Darwinism) and reliance on popular or general web sources detract from authority. Engagement with critical scholarship is present but limited, so application of sources is only partially effective. Overall quality aligns with the mid-high (3-4) band; a score of 4 is appropriate.

Throughout the analysis the student employs literary vocabularyâsymbolism, enjambment, diction, pathos, hyperbole, juxtapositionâand identifies devices accurately in most cases. This indicates a competent grasp of subject-specific terminology.  

Nevertheless, there are moments of imprecision or misapplication (e.g., confusing diction with vulgarity, describing enjambment as ânot using a pause throughout a poemâ without full nuance). Such slips suggest command is good but not consistently secure. The work therefore fits the 3-4 band, and a mark of 4 recognises its generally sound but not flawless use of terminology.

The research question ("How do Kendrick Lamar and Nazrul Islam explore the theme of liberation through their respective lyricism and poetry?") is concise, sharply delimited, and tightly linked to the ensuing analysis of women and culture. Each major section explicitly addresses aspects of the question, demonstrating a sustained conceptual thread.  

Some digressionsâe.g., biographical detail that is not always tied back to the analytical lineâslightly weaken the precision of the focus, so the work does not fully exemplify the highest level of sophistication. Still, the clarity and appropriateness of the RQ place it firmly within band 5-6.

Footer remains blank; add page numbering to comply with IB formatting guidelines.
Consistently missing footer page numbers; add them to meet IB formatting requirements.

Bibliographic entries lack a consistent citation style and some website sources are missing publisher or date details; apply a uniform academic format.
The footer on this page is unnumbered; include the page number to maintain sequential formatting.

Footer missing page number; include sequential numbering on every page to comply with IB guidelines.
This section is cut off mid-idea; ensure all sentences are complete to preserve the coherence of the analysis.
Nuanced interpretation of Lamarâs lyricism (âMy auntie is a man nowâ) demonstrates critical engagement with controversial readings.
Page number is missing in the footer; add sequential numbering on every page.
This subheading lacks the consistent bold styling used elsewhere; standardize heading formats throughout.

Footer remains unnumbered; include the page marker to maintain consistent IB formatting.
Religious Allusion subheading formatting deviates from earlier sections; ensure consistent styling for each analytic category.
Citation of âbible.com, 1996â is incomplete and not in a recognized academic style; revise bibliography entry for full bibliographic detail.
Footer is missing a page number; ensure each page is numbered to fulfill IB document requirements.
The conclusion restates key points well but lacks reflection on limitations or counter-arguments; integrate evaluative critique to strengthen closure.
The title is overly long and vague; consider shortening it and specifying the exact texts and lens to sharpen focus and immediately convey the precise scope of the investigation.
The main heading clearly identifies this document as an Extended Essay, meeting IB structural conventions and orienting the reader effectively.
Your analysis of symbolism remains largely descriptive; push deeper to show how this device specifically develops the theme of liberation.
The interpretation of the âmotherâ metaphor effectively conveys pathos and demonstrates solid use of subject-specific terminology.
This paragraph is overly long and dense; divide it into smaller paragraphs to enhance readability and maintain analytic focus.
The heading clearly signals the shift to âPortrayal of Culture,â aiding reader navigation through comparative dimensions.
Definition of intersectionality relies on a single web source; strengthen your contextual framework by citing peer-reviewed scholarship.
This passage offers a strong thematic synthesis, demonstrating sophisticated comparative thinking across both authorsâ cultural portrayals.
The conclusion heading is clearly signposted, effectively signaling the final synthesis and answer to your research question.
The word count is provided and remains under the 4,000-word limit, satisfying formatting requirements.
Page numbers are missing from the footer; include them consistently to aid navigation and referencing.
Leader dots in the table of contents are misaligned and uneven; standardize dot-leader formatting for clearer navigation.
Page numbers are absent in the footer; adding them will fulfill IB administrative formatting requirements.
The opening paragraph provides broad historical context but does not yet tie directly to your chosen texts; tighten this to establish immediate relevance.
Citation style is inconsistentâpage numbers and authorâdate formats are missing; standardize in-text citations to a single scholarly style.
Relying on a general dictionary for the definition of âliberationâ limits theoretical rigor; incorporate a literary studies or cultural theory source for depth.
Typographical error: âyolk of British imperialismâ should read âyokeâ; correct to maintain factual and conceptual accuracy.
The extensive biographical orientation lacks explicit rationale for text selection; clarify why these authors and works were chosen and how they will be analyzed.
The paragraph ends mid-sentence, disrupting flow; ensure all sections conclude fully for reader comprehension.
The claim that Kendrick Lamar won a Pulitzer Prize in 2018 is made without citation; provide a scholarly or primary source to substantiate this historical point.
This introductory paragraph repeats earlier rationales; consider merging or refining to eliminate redundancy and improve structural coherence.
Use of a clear subheading (âSymbolismâ) effectively organizes your comparative analysis and guides the reader through each device.
Heading levels are inconsistent in hierarchy; use a uniform heading style to reflect your document structure clearly.
Your hyperbole analysis (âWe ain't know no betterâ¦â) would benefit from explicitly tying this to the overarching argument on liberation.
Footer lacks page number; include consistent numbering to comply with formatting standards.
Footer remains blank on this page; add the page number to maintain consistent formatting.
The overview subsection ends mid-sentence; complete your thoughts here or relocate this summary to the end of the section for clarity.
Footer lacks a page number; include it to satisfy IB formatting requirements.
The comparative discussion here is mainly descriptive; develop more supported analytical claims and link them explicitly to your research question.
In-section âOverviewâ headings disrupt analytic flow; consider summarizing analysis at the end rather than interrupting it.
The initial sentence in this section is wordy and uses a passive construction; revise for concision and active voice to improve readability.
